problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
mechanism: Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type
hypothesis: Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type may resolve Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 21 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
